must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume mentions 'Cross-Functional Collaboration' and developer mentorship of 10+ developers, but no embedded/automotive team context.

reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a Salesforce Platform Manager and DevOps Engineer with 6+ years in CRM release orchestration, Copado, Apex, and LWC. The JD is for a Senior SW Developer requiring deep embedded C, MISRA, Autosar, RTOS, and RADAR domain expertise — all of which are completely absent from this resume. Not a single line of this candidate's experience touches automotive, embedded systems, or signal processing. The only marginal overlap is soft-skills collaboration, which is insufficient to justify a conversation. Do not shortlist — this is a domain mismatch, not a skill gap. Move on immediately.
strengths: COPADO RELEASE MASTERY: Managed 50+ end-to-end Salesforce releases at HSBC across 5 environments with zero unplanned production outages over 18 months — a high-signal production ownership credential, but entirely irrelevant to this embedded C/RTOS role.

SALESFORCE DEVOPS DEPTH: Expert-level CI/CD pipeline architecture using Copado, Gearset, and Flosum across healthcare, banking, and financial services — strong in its own domain, zero transferability to automotive embedded development.

ENTERPRISE SCALE DELIVERY: Reduced deployment cycle time by 35% at FinSure and production defect escape rate by 30% at CVS Health — quantified outcomes demonstrating senior-level impact, but in a completely unrelated technology stack.
gaps: ZERO EMBEDDED/AUTOMOTIVE DOMAIN EXPERIENCE: The role mandates C programming, MISRA guidelines, Autosar, and RTOS — none of these appear anywhere in the resume. This is not a trainable gap at Senior level; it is a fundamental domain mismatch that disqualifies the candidate outright.

NO RADAR OR ADAS EXPOSURE: The JD lists RADAR system knowledge and ADAS as key requirements. The candidate has no signal processing, radar algorithm, or advanced driver-assistance background — their entire career is in enterprise CRM SaaS, making this gap unbridgeable for this hiring cycle.

scoring_factors.reason: Latest role end-date is March 2026 — candidate appears available now, no notice period penalty.
JD requires In-office presence in Chennai; candidate is based in Hyderabad — location mismatch.
Candidate has 6+ years total experience, exceeding the minimum threshold of half of the 4-year relevant experience requirement.
JD gender preference is 'Any' — no penalty applied.
No college exclusion or preference specified in the JD — no adjustment applied.

hard_filter_failures: Location mismatch — candidate is in Hyderabad, role is In-office in Chennai
availability_reason: Latest role end-date is March 2026 — candidate is currently between roles.
alternative_role_fit: Senior Salesforce DevOps Engineer or Salesforce Platform Manager
rare_assets: Zero-finding compliance audit record across 18 months at HSBC — a rare production governance credential in enterprise banking Salesforce.
Federated multi-region branching strategy architecture across parallel global development teams — uncommon at this level.
Center of Excellence establishment adopted as NTT Data standard across multiple Salesforce engagements.
self_reported_unverified: Claim of 'zero unplanned production outages over 18 months' at HSBC — significant claim with no third-party corroboration or supporting incident log reference.
Claim of 'zero-finding results on all internal and external banking compliance audits' — impressive but unverifiable from the resume alone.
'Adopted as the standard across NTT Data Salesforce engagements' — broad organisational impact claim with no supporting evidence such as stakeholder sign-off or documented rollout scope.
interview_flags: Resume end-date is March 2026 — ask why the candidate left or was exited from NTT Data after 6+ years and what prompted the job search.
Probe whether the candidate has any embedded C or systems programming background whatsoever, even academic or hobbyist, given the complete absence on the resume.
Ask about the B.Com degree — a commerce degree is an unusual background for a senior technical developer; probe how they transitioned and whether any CS fundamentals exist.
The LinkedIn URL suggests the name 'Pavan Gupta' but the resume header says 'Pavan Gajjala' — clarify the name discrepancy before any formal submission.
Verify the scope of 'managing 50+ releases' at HSBC — probe whether this was solo ownership or part of a larger release management team to assess actual individual accountability.
